INTC.title: AMD, Arm Stocks Ride Intel's Coattails After Blowout Q1 Report

SAP.title: SAP Shares Climb After Mixed Q1 Report: Details

macro_events.event_summary: Trump warns Americans to expect higher gas prices "for a little while," referencing Iran war and Strait of Hormuz tensions — signaling sustained oil supply risk premium.
primary_drivers: Crude oil supply disruption risk premium from Strait of Hormuz / Iran conflict
Higher refined product crack spreads passed to consumers
Presidential acknowledgment removes near-term political pressure on producers to cap prices

beneficiaries.rationale: Integrated major with large upstream exposure; every $10 sustained Brent move adds material EPS. Presidential signaling that high prices will persist removes SPR-release / jawboning overhang.
Same upstream leverage as XOM; US-domiciled, no Iranian/Russian asset risk. Direct beneficiary of sustained Brent premium with political cover.
Permian-weighted pure-play E&P with high oil (vs gas) mix and operational leverage; among highest beta to WTI in large-cap E&P.
Largest US refiner by capacity; Hormuz disruption widens light-heavy and Brent-WTI spreads, boosting crack margins. Higher retail gasoline prices flow through Speedway/retail segment.
Gulf Coast refiner with complex refineries that benefit from sour crude discount widening if Mideast flows disrupted; gasoline crack expansion is direct revenue driver.
Oilfield services leveraged to US onshore drilling response; sustained >$80 WTI typically triggers completion activity pickup within 1-2 quarters.
Permian pure-play with low breakevens; strong FCF torque at elevated oil with variable dividend framework returning cash directly to holders.

losers.rationale: Jet fuel is ~25-30% of operating costs; sustained crude spike with limited hedging compresses margins. Delta historically under-hedged vs peers.
Unhedged jet fuel exposure by policy; highly levered balance sheet magnifies margin compression from sustained fuel cost increase.
Same jet fuel cost passthrough issue; international route mix means both Brent-linked fuel costs and geopolitical demand softness (Mideast routes).
Domestic leisure carrier most exposed to price-sensitive consumer; higher pump prices dampen discretionary travel demand in addition to direct fuel cost hit.
Ground and air fuel surcharges lag spot prices; Express segment jet fuel exposure meaningful. Consumer freight demand also pressured by gasoline-tax-like hit to wallets.
Lower-income consumer base disproportionately hit by gasoline as share of wallet; transportation/logistics fuel costs compress already-thin grocery margins.
Bunker fuel is major cost input and cruise demand is discretionary; sustained oil spike hits both cost and demand sides simultaneously.

headlines: Trump says Americans should expect higher gas prices for 'a little while'

macro_events.event_summary: Trump administration moves to reclassify marijuana (likely from Schedule I to Schedule III under the Controlled Substances Act), which would remove 280E tax penalties and ease banking/research restrictions for cannabis operators.
primary_drivers: Removal of IRC Section 280E tax burden, dramatically improving cash flow and net income for plant-touching US cannabis operators
Reduced federal legal overhang expands institutional investor access and potential uplisting to major exchanges
Broader normalization path increases addressable market and M&A optionality

beneficiaries.rationale: Canopy Growth — holds Canopy USA structure with options on US MSOs (Wana, Jetty, Acreage); Schedule III reduces the friction of exercising that US optionality.
Tilray — has built US-adjacent platform (beverages, hemp) and explicit cannabis US optionality; benefits from sentiment and flows into the few Nasdaq-listed cannabis names that retail can access.
Scotts Miracle-Gro via Hawthorne hydroponics subsidiary — direct revenue exposure to US cultivation capex cycle, which accelerates if federal reclassification spurs expansion.
Innovative Industrial Properties — cannabis REIT whose tenants (MSOs) see improved credit quality post-280E; lower tenant default risk supports dividend and re-rating.

losers.rationale: Constellation Brands — beer/alcohol franchise (Modelo, Corona) faces incremental long-term share loss as cannabis normalization accelerates cannabis-beverage substitution, particularly among younger consumers. Second-order and slow-moving.
Anheuser-Busch InBev — same alcohol-substitution thesis as STZ; any federal cannabis normalization is a modest long-term headwind to US beer volumes.

headlines: What the Trump administration's move to reclassify marijuana means for investors

macro_events.event_summary: Cannabis rescheduling announcement triggers a "sell the news" reaction despite being a long-awaited regulatory positive for the industry.
primary_drivers: Schedule III reclassification removes IRS 280E tax penalty, materially improving operator cash flow
Sell-the-news profit-taking after multi-month run-up into the catalyst
Residual uncertainty on implementation timeline and interstate commerce/banking implications

beneficiaries.rationale: Cronos has a large cash position and Canadian operations; rescheduling in the US opens potential pathway for Canadian LPs to eventually enter US market via cross-border M&A as federal stigma eases.

losers.rationale: Altria's growth optionality partly rests on alternative nicotine/cannabis plays; a legitimized cannabis market with tax-advantaged MSOs becomes a more credible competitor for share of wallet versus tobacco, though exposure is modest.

Constellation Brands owns large stake in Canopy Growth (CGC) — but Canopy is structured around US access via Canopy USA; near-term read is mixed, and alcohol category faces incremental substitution risk from normalized cannabis.

headlines: The cannabis rescheduling is causing celebration — and confusion — as marijuana stocks sell off
